sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after frames to see what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before.segmentation['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after.segmentation['nodes']}

# Check for color/pixel/boundary changes
print("=== Checking for changes ===")
for nid in set(list(before_nodes.keys()) + list(after_nodes.keys())):
    bn = before_nodes.get(nid)
    an = after_nodes.get(nid)
    if bn is None:
        print(f"New node id={nid}, color={an['color']}, pixels={an['pixels']}")
    elif an is None:
        print(f"Removed node id={nid}, color={bn['color']}, pixels={bn['pixels']}")
    elif bn['pixels'] != an['pixels'] or bn['color'] != an['color']:
        print(f"Changed id={nid}: {bn['color']}/{bn['pixels']} -> {an['color']}/{an['pixels']}")
    elif bn['boundary'] != an['boundary']:
        print(f"Moved id={nid}: {bn['boundary']} -> {an['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Interesting! Clicking the red cross at (10, 52) changed:
- id=29: G/15 -> G/14 (dark gray, decreased by 1 pixel)
- id=30: c/49 -> c/50 (charcoal, increased by 1 pixel)
So the dark gray object lost a pixel and the charcoal object gained a pixel. This suggests clicking the red cross transfers a pixel from the dark gray object to the charcoal object. But what are these objects? Let me check their boundaries and positions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what id=29 and id=30 are
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['id'] in (29, 30):
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}, children={n['children']}")

# Also check all objects with their boundaries for context
print("\n=== All objects ===")
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 10:
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
